q: How much does ChatGPT cost per month?
a: ChatGPT Free is $0. ChatGPT Plus is $20/month and gives access to GPT-4o with rate limits. ChatGPT Team is $25-30/user/month. The API is pay-per-token — GPT-4o costs $5/1M input tokens and $15/1M output tokens.

Claude 3.5 Sonnet has cheaper input tokens ($3 vs $5 per 1M) than GPT-4o, with equal output pricing ($15/1M). Claude Pro and ChatGPT Plus both cost $20/month for subscriptions. Budget models: Claude Haiku is more expensive per token than GPT-4o mini.

q: What is the cheapest AI model available?
a: Gemini 1.5 Flash is among the cheapest at $0.075/1M input tokens. GPT-4o mini is $0.15/1M input. Mistral Small is $0.20/1M input. For free options: Groq's Llama 3.1 70B has a free API tier.

q: How many tokens is a typical conversation?
a: A short message exchange (5-10 turns): 500-2000 tokens total. A longer conversation: 3,000-10,000 tokens. A typical blog post: ~750 tokens. 1 token ≈ 0.75 words ≈ 4 characters of English text.

q: When should I use the API instead of a subscription?
a: Use the API if you're building a product, running automated workflows, or want to optimize costs by using cheaper models. Use a subscription if you primarily chat interactively and don't need programmatic access. The API with GPT-4o mini is often cheaper than ChatGPT Plus for moderate usage.

For professional developers: almost certainly yes. Cursor Pro includes frontier model access (Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT-4o) embedded in your editor with codebase awareness. Even a 10% productivity gain pays for the subscription in under a day of work.

q: What AI model is best for coding?
a: Claude 3.5 Sonnet leads most 2025 coding benchmarks. GPT-4o is close behind. For budget coding: Claude 3.5 Haiku. For integrated coding experience: Cursor Pro (which uses these models). For free: Cursor Free or GitHub Copilot Free.

q: Can I use DeepSeek instead of GPT-4o to save money?
a: Yes — DeepSeek V3 is 95% cheaper than GPT-4o and performs competitively on most benchmarks. Main caveats: it's a Chinese company (data privacy considerations), latency is higher, and availability can be inconsistent. For internal tools without data sensitivity requirements, it's an excellent choice.

q: How do I estimate my monthly AI API cost?
a: Use our cost calculator. You need: average input tokens per request, average output tokens per request, and requests per month. Multiply each by the model's per-token price and sum them. Most users are surprised at how cheap budget models are — GPT-4o mini typically costs under $5/month at moderate usage.

q: Is there a free alternative to ChatGPT Plus?
a: Yes. ChatGPT Free itself is capable for most uses. Gemini Free offers similar capabilities. Claude.ai has a free tier with limited Sonnet access. Groq offers free API access to Llama 3.1 70B. For coding, Cursor Free and GitHub Copilot Free both provide meaningful free access.

q: What is model routing and how does it save money?
a: Model routing means sending different requests to different AI models based on task complexity. Simple tasks (classification, Q&A, summaries) go to cheap models like GPT-4o mini. Complex tasks go to expensive models like GPT-4o. This can cut AI costs by 60-80% with minimal quality loss.

q: Are AI subscriptions worth it compared to free tiers?
a: For light users: probably not. Free tiers from ChatGPT and Gemini cover occasional use. For moderate-to-heavy users: yes — rate limits on free tiers become frustrating, and $20/month for high-quality access is reasonable. For developers: the API is usually more cost-effective than subscriptions at scale.
